lesson: A simple challenger can dominate or falsify a proposed method, so baseline-dominance checks must be mandatory.
Duplicate and zero-dimension records are useful data-quality signals but do not make the entire dataset unusable.
Duplicate rows matter, but pandas can produce the raw finding; tool value is evidence packaging.
Dummy and shuffled-label controls are valuable, but one split cannot certify a benchmark protocol.
Static test inventory is useful only when reconciled against runtime collection and execution.
Tool reuse evidence is only credible when failures and downgraded targets stay visible.
The rejected class-weight extension prevented an unsupported improvement claim.
Seven of eight class-weight extensions were rejected or downgraded, and Vehicle fallback showed target access can fail honestly.
